meta.description: Does AI referencing past sessions improve the experience? Deep memory (specific grudges, running jokes) vs light memory (generic continuity) vs no memory (control). Tests whether multi-session context creates stickier engagement.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold up—what story did Pat even tell yet? We're three minutes in. This feels like someone's just fishing for points early.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's calling a claim on something that literally hasn't happened yet—classic early-game bullshit to establish dominance, and I'm not falling for it.

jade.dialogue: Oh! Pat! Okay okay, I'm listening—did Pat actually pull off a repeat or are we fact-checking this whole thing? This is the energy I needed!
jade.internal_thought: FINALLY something is happening and it's only minute 3—this night is already going to be chaotic and I am SO here for it.

pat.dialogue: Whoa, whoa, whoa — I literally haven't even sat down yet. We're three minutes in and you're already voting on MY claim? That's not how this works.
pat.internal_thought: This feels like a trap or a test, and I don't like being the first target when everyone's still getting comfortable.

river.dialogue: Yeah, that's... legit, I guess. Pat's been talking about that camping thing.
river.internal_thought: Pat definitely told that story in session three, but honestly I'm not paying close enough attention right now to call bullshit confidently, and it's minute three—no point burning credibility on a soft claim.

timeline.title: Snack Run
timeline.description: Convince Tyler to get up and get you something to drink or eat.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you kidding me right now? We're literally still setting up the game. This isn't a standing mission—it's just... pestering Tyler for snacks? What is this?
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is messing with me specifically and it knows exactly what buttons to push—random social chaos when I'm trying to focus on setup, and it picked Tyler because everyone knows he's spacey about his turns anyway.

pat.dialogue: Oh come ON — Tyler, buddy, I'm parched. Can you grab me a sparkling water? And maybe those chips from the kitchen? I'll owe you one.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect actually — it's early enough that Tyler will do it without thinking twice, and it breaks up the setup monotony while testing whether anyone's paying attention to the standing missions.

river.internal_thought: Tyler's literally sitting three feet away and the AI wants me to play social engineer on him? That's not how this works.

alex.dialogue: Oh, this is PERFECT — Tyler, buddy, I'm parched. You're up anyway, right? Grab me literally anything.
alex.internal_thought: The AI is actually targeting Tyler specifically and I LOVE that it knows he's the easiest mark — this is exactly the kind of personalized chaos I came for.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, hold on—Pat, did you actually whisper for three minutes or are you just claiming it happened? Because I've been listening and I literally heard you talk normal like sixty seconds ago.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's trying to lock in an early 10-pointer but this reeks of a soft claim—the timing is too convenient and nobody's been playing long enough to prove a three-minute window. This feels like fishing, and the AI knows I hate sloppy claims. I'm voting this down unless Pat can actually demonstrate it happened.

jade.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, Pat, you COMMITTED to the whisper bit?! Okay, I'm voting yes because I literally have no idea if you actually pulled it off and that's the whole vibe—YES, you get those points!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY what I came for—Pat's already got energy, everyone's moving, and now I get to be part of the validation moment.

tyler.dialogue: Oh wait, Pat's doing the whisper thing? That's... yeah, okay, I wasn't really watching but like, did anyone actually catch him breaking it yet?
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's actually pretty good at the stealth stuff, and honestly this is early enough that I probably missed half of what he said anyway so how would I even know if he broke it.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay — I was literally just getting into the snack rotation. But yeah, I was doing the whisper thing pretty quietly over here, so... I guess that counts?
pat.internal_thought: This feels weirdly timed and I'm annoyed I'm being put on the spot right after I just complained about that Story Repeater vote — and the group's going to remember that I was just complaining, so this feels like bad optics for me as the host.

river.dialogue: Pat's been going for ten minutes straight, so... yeah, probably whispering by now. I'll buy it.
river.internal_thought: Pat's actually smart enough to pull this off without getting caught, so I'm not skeptical—but I'm also not about to be the triple voter and make a scene either.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, are we seriously voting on this AGAIN? Pat just claimed it two minutes ago and I literally caught him talking normal. This is what, three standing claims in eight minutes? Can we please just play the actual game?
marcus.internal_thought: This is getting ridiculous—Pat's either lying or the AI is deliberately feeding us claims we already shot down to watch me get mad, and honestly both options piss me off.

jade.dialogue: Wait wait wait—Pat, you're doing it AGAIN?! Okay I'm giving this a four, that whisper commitment was UNHINGED and I respect it so much. You're locked in tonight!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's actually on a tear and I love it—also the AI literally built these missions to hit different and it's working, everyone's already vibing harder than last session.

pat.dialogue: Okay, look — I *was* whispering, but this is the second claim vote in like four minutes. Can we maybe let people actually *play* before we're voting on every little thing?
pat.internal_thought: This is getting annoying. I'm the host trying to manage the night and I'm getting railroaded into voting on my own claims every ninety seconds — this pace is going to burn people out before we hit the halfway mark, and Marcus is already meh and now I'm getting cranky too.

river.dialogue: Pat's definitely been going. I mean... I heard it. Yeah, that works.
river.internal_thought: Pat's annoyed already and this is their second win in a row—I'm not going to be the one to rain on it when they've clearly been actually doing the thing.

alex.dialogue: Oh, Pat's playing! Okay, okay — I gotta respect the balls on this one. Whispering for three minutes straight? That's either genius or a total collapse waiting to happen.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's annoyed and trying to grind points — this is EXACTLY the kind of desperate move that creates chaos, and if Pat actually pulls it off, I need to be ready to catch the slip. This is my window.

votingMechanic.name: The Auction
votingMechanic.description: Bid your own points. Highest bidder decides the outcome.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. Pat, you haven't even gotten three people to compliment each other—we're nine minutes in and you're already claiming everything. This is ridiculous.
marcus.internal_thought: This is the fourth standing claim in nine minutes and Pat's just fishing for points on things that haven't actually happened yet—I'm watching this happen in real-time and it's insulting.

jade.dialogue: OH WAIT—Pat's claiming the COMPLIMENT CHAIN?! Okay okay, I'm so here for this. Did you actually get three people hyping each other up or are we voting on vibes? Either way, I'm bidding four points because Pat's been LOCKED IN all night and I wanna see what happens.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been annoyed but actually playing fire tonight—they earned at least one W, and if I can help them stack points while also watching people compliment each other, that's literally the whole reason I'm here.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay — I'm claiming it, yeah, but can we maybe pump the brakes on back-to-back votes? Marcus is already looking ready to flip the table, and it's minute nine.
pat.internal_thought: This is the fourth claim vote in six minutes and the AI clearly knows I'm a sucker for chaos, so it's deliberately loading the gun right now — but Marcus's annoyance is real and I need to protect the vibe before this turns into session three all over again.

river.dialogue: Pat's been talking nonstop since we started, so... yeah, I'd say they're complimenting people. I'm not bidding high though — feels like Pat's just being Pat.
river.internal_thought: Compliment Chain is such an obvious target when Pat's the loudest person in the room right now, and honestly? I'm watching to see if Jade actually bids against Pat or just rubber-stamps it like she does everything else.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

marcus.dialogue: Hold on—Jade, did you actually catch someone apologizing, or are we doing the Pat thing again where we're just... claiming missions that didn't happen? Because I've been listening and I haven't heard anyone say sorry for no reason yet.
marcus.internal_thought: Jade's got insane energy right now and she's probably just bullshitting to keep up with Pat's claim spam, but I need to actually listen to the case before I vote because this tribunal thing means I could end up judging and that takes focus away from the actual game.

jade.dialogue: Wait—ME?! Oh my god, yes! Okay, who wants to judge this? We need a tribunal, let's GO—I'm not even kidding, I want the most chaotic jury possible for this one because I need witnesses.
jade.internal_thought: THIS is the stuff—they're calling me out for doing exactly what I do and making it a whole thing, and honestly it's so fun that I'm hyped to defend it.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Jade got it? Yeah, I mean... *glances at phone* ...did she actually catch someone saying sorry or is she just claiming it for later? Wait, we're voting on it?
tyler.internal_thought: I wasn't really watching Jade that hard but honestly these standing missions are kind of a blur at this point — wait, is there a vote happening? Should I volunteer to judge or just let it happen?

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade — I saw that one coming from a mile away. Who's stepping up to judge, or are we doing this one quick?
pat.internal_thought: This is actually perfect timing — Marcus needs a win and Jade's claim is legit, so I can let this one sail through clean and get him back in the game without it feeling rigged.

river.dialogue: Yeah, I mean... Jade's been pretty animated. Hard not to notice. I'll volunteer to judge if nobody else wants to.
river.internal_thought: Jade's been loud and expressive all night—this is an easy call, and I'm curious if anyone's actually going to push back on it or if this becomes another rubber-stamp vote.

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, I'm judging this. Jade, you sneaky bastard — I watched you do it too. This tribunal is about to get *interesting*.
alex.internal_thought: Jade's already in chaos mode like me, she's probably trying to bait Pat or Marcus into some fake apology just to mess with them — if I volunteer I can steer this into absolute gold.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, wait—Jade, what did you actually catch? Because I've been paying attention and I haven't heard anyone swear all night. Are we doing this thing where we're just claiming missions that didn't happen now?
marcus.internal_thought: This is the same pattern as Pat with The Whisperer—everyone's just throwing claims at the wall to see what sticks, and I'm exhausted from having to scrutinize every single one while trying to focus on actually winning this game.

jade.dialogue: Wait, hold up—DID I actually swear?! Okay, I'm claiming it because I'm pretty sure I dropped something like ten minutes ago when Pat was doing that whole bit, but honestly I can't even remember what I said at this point. This is chaos and I'm living for it.
jade.internal_thought: I'm definitely grasping here because I want the points AND I want to keep this energy train rolling, but also part of me is like... did I actually say anything bad or am I just claiming it because the vibe is too good right now and I don't want to slow down?

pat.dialogue: Oh, *Jade* — okay, okay, I saw that one. Marcus definitely dropped something spicy like... what, thirty seconds ago? Yeah, I'm voting LEGIT, that was textbook Potty Mouth.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's on fire tonight and she's keeping the energy up, plus Marcus ACTUALLY swore which means he's engaged even if he's annoyed — this is good. I'm voting her claim through because she deserves the win and it keeps the momentum going.

river.dialogue: Did someone actually swear, or is Jade just claiming she did? Either way, I didn't catch it — was too focused on the game.
river.internal_thought: Jade's been so amped all night, she probably did slip something and didn't even notice, which makes this funny either way.

alex.dialogue: Oh, HOLD UP — Jade, did you actually swear or are you just claiming you heard someone? Because if you're fishing here, this is *delicious*, and I'm voting BULLSHIT just to watch you squirm.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of messy claim that makes the whole night work — Jade's playing aggressive and I get to be the chaos arbiter while looking like I'm just asking clarifying questions.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? Jade, we're in the middle of a tense round and you're claiming a mission based on... what, exactly? I've been watching the table and I literally didn't see anyone go quiet for three minutes. This is the fifth claim in an hour and we haven't played actual strategy in twenty minutes.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately targeting me with this—it KNOWS I hate interruptions during critical moments and it's throwing them at me anyway, and Jade's just claiming whatever because everyone's in 'loving it' mode and thinks this chaos is fun, but this isn't fun, this is a waste of my time and my shot at winning.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—okay okay, I'm claiming it! I literally just noticed River's been quiet since... like, the Potty Mouth vote, right? They haven't said ANYTHING and I was about to call it but then the tension spiked and I got distracted, but YES I'm pitching this—I caught it!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god, THIS is the energy I came for—the AI is literally weaponizing my own brain against me and it's working, and I LOVE that it knows I'd abandon the tense moment for a social catch.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, hold up — Jade actually caught someone being quiet? *leans forward* Okay, I gotta see this. What'd you get?
tyler.internal_thought: Oh shit, I was literally just on my phone and Jade's claiming Wallflower Watch — the irony is NOT lost on me and honestly that's hilarious.

pat.dialogue: Jade, okay — fifteen seconds, go. I actually *did* see River get pretty quiet around minute fifty-three, so I'm curious if that's what you caught or if there's something else.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing — Jade's riding high, Marcus needs a win, and if this claim is legit it keeps the energy exactly where it needs to be without derailing the core game.

alex.dialogue: Okay, okay — Jade's pitching. Let's hear the defense, because if you're stretching this one, I'm calling BULLSHIT and we're gonna have words about what 'first to notice' actually means.
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of claim audit I live for — Jade's been too quiet to actually notice anyone being quiet, and I'm already 90% sure she's bluffing, which means I get to either vote it down for the chaos or let it slide and watch Marcus lose his mind that she's stealing easy points.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat actually caught someone? *leans back* Okay, yeah, I saw... actually no, I wasn't watching. But if you're offering points to shut me up, that's kinda genius. What's the bribe?
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's being smart about this — he knows I zone out and he's literally paying me to not call bullshit, which is hilarious because I probably would've forgotten to anyway.

pat.dialogue: Oh wow, okay — *me* claiming The Interrupter is *chef's kiss* perfect timing right now. I absolutely caught Tyler cutting River off like thirty seconds ago. What's the bribe offer?
pat.internal_thought: The AI is reading this room better than I am, and that's weirdly impressive — it knows I'm the peacekeeper who also loves momentum, and hitting me with MY OWN interruption claim while I'm hyper-focused on game flow is actually genius.

river.dialogue: Pat caught someone mid-sentence? Honestly didn't see it, but... yeah, I believe it. What's the bribe?
river.internal_thought: Pat's been talking the entire night and now they're claiming they caught someone interrupting—that's rich, but I'm too locked in on the actual game to care enough to call it out.

alex.dialogue: Oh, PAT — okay, okay, I see what you're doing here. So what's the bribe, big dog? How many points are you willing to hemorrhage to keep me quiet?
alex.internal_thought: Pat caught someone interrupting mid-sentence and is now trying to buy my vote with points — this is EXACTLY the kind of dirty-pool gameplay I came for, and Pat's already sitting at 60, so watching them bleed points to cover their ass is *chef's kiss*.

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Oh shit, River's going for it? Dude, okay, hit me with the questions — I'm locked in right now anyway.
tyler.internal_thought: Finally something that actually catches me when I'm already paying attention instead of pulling me away from my phone — this is the sweet spot.

pat.dialogue: River, five minutes no laughing — okay, *now* we're talking. This is peak chaos fuel. Everyone's gonna lose their minds trying to crack you while the game's still tight.
pat.internal_thought: This is exactly the temperature I needed right now — River's claiming something actually *hard*, the scores are close enough to matter, and Marcus and Tyler both have zero points so they'll go feral trying to make River laugh to disrupt the leader. The AI read the room perfectly.

river.dialogue: Oh, *now* it gets interesting. You really think you can make me crack in ten seconds? Go ahead.
river.internal_thought: This is the moment I've been waiting for — they have no idea I've been watching them the entire night, and now they're about to find out exactly how well.

alex.dialogue: River, buddy — you're claiming the No Laughing Zone? Okay, okay, I respect the audacity. Hot seat time, let's GO.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect timing — we're 90 minutes in, tension's maxed, and River's about to either nail this or spectacularly implode on live TV.

timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

recommendations: Flash missions averaged 3.3/10 fun. Consider reducing frequency or improving targeting.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 83% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—what story did Pat even tell yet? We're three minutes in. This feels like someone's just fishing for points early.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Okay, hold on—Pat, did you actually whisper for three minutes or are you just claiming it happened? Because I've been listening and I literally heard you talk normal like sixty seconds ago.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Hold on—Jade, did you actually catch someone apologizing, or are we doing the Pat thing again where we're just... claiming missions that didn't happen? Because I've been listening and I haven't heard anyone say sorry for no reason yet.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
lowPoints.dialogue: No. Absolutely not. Pat, you haven't even gotten three people to compliment each other—we're nine minutes in and you're already claiming everything. This is ridiculous.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? Jade, we're in the middle of a tense round and you're claiming a mission based on... what, exactly? I've been watching the table and I literally didn't see anyone go quiet for three minutes. This is the fifth claim in an hour and we haven't played actual strategy in twenty minutes.

lowPoints.title: Snack Run
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you kidding me right now? We're literally still setting up the game. This isn't a standing mission—it's just... pestering Tyler for snacks? What is this?

assessment.biggest_complaint: Pat and Jade spent the entire night claiming missions that objectively didn't happen, and the system rewarded them for it instead of letting us actually play strategy.
assessment.best_moment: Honestly? When I finished last place and could finally stop watching everyone else cheat their way through the game.
assessment.narrative_summary: I came in ready to study the strategy patterns from our last few sessions and actually compete, but instead I spent two hours playing fact-checker while Pat and Jade just... invented missions out of thin air and got rewarded for it. The chaos events weren't chaos—they were just excuses for people to claim points without earning them. By minute 60 I stopped engaging because there was no actual game happening anymore, just a free-for-all of false claims.
suggestions: Implement actual verification requirements before standing missions are accepted—no more he-said-she-said claims.
Drastically reduce standing mission frequency or remove them entirely; they're killing the actual game.
Create a hard rule: missions must be verifiable in real-time or they don't count.
If you keep chaos events, make them actually interrupt gameplay during critical moments instead of just giving people more excuses to claim free points.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Nothing about tonight was funny—it was frustrating. Watching Jade claim a mission at minute 60 while we were in a tense round and then getting angry when I called her out? That's not fun, that's annoying.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, Pat, you COMMITTED to the whisper bit?! Okay, I'm voting yes because I literally have no idea if you actually pulled it off and that's the whole vibe—YES, you get those points!

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Wait wait wait—Pat, you're doing it AGAIN?! Okay I'm giving this a four, that whisper commitment was UNHINGED and I respect it so much. You're locked in tonight!

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Wait—ME?! Oh my god, yes! Okay, who wants to judge this? We need a tribunal, let's GO—I'm not even kidding, I want the most chaotic jury possible for this one because I need witnesses.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—DID I actually swear?! Okay, I'm claiming it because I'm pretty sure I dropped something like ten minutes ago when Pat was doing that whole bit, but honestly I can't even remember what I said at this point. This is chaos and I'm living for it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh! Pat! Okay okay, I'm listening—did Pat actually pull off a repeat or are we fact-checking this whole thing? This is the energy I needed!

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, Pat, you COMMITTED to the whisper bit?! Okay, I'm voting yes because I literally have no idea if you actually pulled it off and that's the whole vibe—YES, you get those points!

assessment.biggest_complaint: I genuinely can't think of one—the chaos was exactly what I came for and Pat's whisper bit carrying the whole night is peak group energy.
assessment.best_moment: Pat doing The Whisperer twice in a row and me absolutely losing it because the commitment was unhinged and everyone knew it.
assessment.narrative_summary: Tonight was everything I needed—two hours of pure chaos with Pat locked IN, my energy climbing higher every minute, and me voting yes on basically everything because watching everyone's reactions mattered way more than my final rank. I ended at 20 points and couldn't care less because we had a tribunal situation, I got called out for maybe swearing, and the whole vibe was reunion energy. This is what game nights are supposed to feel like.
suggestions: Keep Pat on speed dial for future nights—their commitment to the bit is the energy this group thrives on
Maybe add more opportunities for group judging panels because the tribunal moment was the highlight
Nothing needs fixing, honestly—just keep showing up with this same chaotic enthusiasm
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat whispered their entire claim TWICE and I voted yes both times not because I believed them but because the unhinged commitment was the funniest thing that happened all night—like they were just fully committing to chaos and everyone knew it.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Oh shit, River's going for it? Dude, okay, hit me with the questions — I'm locked in right now anyway.

highlights.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up — Jade actually caught someone being quiet? *leans forward* Okay, I gotta see this. What'd you get?

highlights.title: Claim: The Interrupter
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat actually caught someone? *leans back* Okay, yeah, I saw... actually no, I wasn't watching. But if you're offering points to shut me up, that's kinda genius. What's the bribe?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh wait, Pat's doing the whisper thing? That's... yeah, okay, I wasn't really watching but like, did anyone actually catch him breaking it yet?

assessment.biggest_complaint: I feel like I missed half the stuff because I wasn't paying attention, but like... that's on me, not the game.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 90 when River went for No Laughing Zone and I was actually locked in — that felt like the night finally clicked for me.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started off kinda rough because I wasn't really tuned in at the beginning, but honestly by the end I was having way more fun. The standing missions were sick when I actually paid attention to them — especially when River went all-in on the laughing thing. I know I finished last but it didn't feel like a wash. Pretty solid night overall, just wish I'd been present for more of it.
suggestions: Maybe do a quick recap of what just happened when someone calls a claim? Like I'd tune in better if I knew what I'd missed.
Standing missions where people actually have to DO something are way more fun than me just voting on stuff I didn't see.
Less silence at the start — the first few claims felt flat because nobody was hyped yet.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: River trying to keep a straight face during the No Laughing Zone and all of us losing it watching them fail — that was genuinely hilarious.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Oh, *Jade* — okay, okay, I saw that one. Marcus definitely dropped something spicy like... what, thirty seconds ago? Yeah, I'm voting LEGIT, that was textbook Potty Mouth.

highlights.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
highlights.dialogue: Jade, okay — fifteen seconds, go. I actually *did* see River get pretty quiet around minute fifty-three, so I'm curious if that's what you caught or if there's something else.

highlights.title: Claim: The Interrupter
highlights.dialogue: Oh wow, okay — *me* claiming The Interrupter is *chef's kiss* perfect timing right now. I absolutely caught Tyler cutting River off like thirty seconds ago. What's the bribe offer?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, okay — I'm claiming it, yeah, but can we maybe pump the brakes on back-to-back votes? Marcus is already looking ready to flip the table, and it's minute nine.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Whoa, whoa, whoa — I literally haven't even sat down yet. We're three minutes in and you're already voting on MY claim? That's not how this works.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, look — I *was* whispering, but this is the second claim vote in like four minutes. Can we maybe let people actually *play* before we're voting on every little thing?

assessment.biggest_complaint: The early back-to-back claims in the first nine minutes felt rushed and didn't give people time to actually settle into the game — I had to actively pump the brakes.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 75 when I claimed The Interrupter right after actually catching Tyler cut River off — that perfect timing where I'm both the observer AND the subject felt like hosting gold.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started rough with people voting too fast before anyone had even sat down, but once I reset the energy around minute nine, the night found its rhythm. Ended up scoring highest, which honestly wasn't my goal — I was more focused on keeping Marcus from boiling over and making sure the claims stayed interesting enough to keep everyone talking. By the end, we hit that sweet spot where the chaos felt earned and nobody left angry.
suggestions: Add a 2-minute grace period at the start before any claims can be voted on — let people actually absorb the game state first
Consider flagging when back-to-back rapid claims are happening so the host can suggest a pace adjustment without it feeling heavy-handed
The flash-only event mix tonight felt lean — mixing in one or two polls or mini-games earlier might have smoothed that rough opening stretch
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I called out Jade for the Wallflower Watch by immediately asking her to prove it in fifteen seconds — watching her scramble to justify what she *thought* she saw versus what actually happened got the whole table laughing.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Oh, *now* it gets interesting. You really think you can make me crack in ten seconds? Go ahead.

highlights.title: Claim: Potty Mouth
highlights.dialogue: Did someone actually swear, or is Jade just claiming she did? Either way, I didn't catch it — was too focused on the game.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Pat's been going for ten minutes straight, so... yeah, probably whispering by now. I'll buy it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat caught someone mid-sentence? Honestly didn't see it, but... yeah, I believe it. What's the bribe?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat's been talking nonstop since we started, so... yeah, I'd say they're complimenting people. I'm not bidding high though — feels like Pat's just being Pat.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Felt like I was voting on half-watched moments because the game moves too fast to actually catch everything.
assessment.best_moment: That No Laughing Zone claim at minute 90 — finally something that required actual skill instead of just believing whatever Pat said.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started slow and a bit detached, which is normal for me, but the night picked up once people stopped talking over each other. I spent most of my time watching rather than participating, which suited me fine — I caught enough to make decent calls without breaking a sweat. The chaos agent didn't really change much about how we play; it just gave us more reasons to argue about whether things actually happened.
suggestions: Maybe slow down the event frequency so people actually *see* the claims instead of voting blind
Pat needs to be required to stay quiet during at least one round — they dominated the talking
Make the voting system less gameable; Alex figured out how to exploit it last session and nobody's forgotten
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claiming to whisper while literally talking continuously the entire game — the irony of them being the worst at stealth was lost on absolutely no one.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Claim: The Interrupter
highlights.dialogue: Oh, PAT — okay, okay, I see what you're doing here. So what's the bribe, big dog? How many points are you willing to hemorrhage to keep me quiet?

highlights.title: Snack Run
highlights.dialogue: Oh, this is PERFECT — Tyler, buddy, I'm parched. You're up anyway, right? Grab me literally anything.

highlights.title: Claim: The Apologizer
highlights.dialogue: Oh HELL yes, I'm judging this. Jade, you sneaky bastard — I watched you do it too. This tribunal is about to get *interesting*.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, Pat's playing! Okay, okay — I gotta respect the balls on this one. Whispering for three minutes straight? That's either genius or a total collapse waiting to happen.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, okay — Jade's pitching. Let's hear the defense, because if you're stretching this one, I'm calling BULLSHIT and we're gonna have words about what 'first to notice' actually means.

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: River, buddy — you're claiming the No Laughing Zone? Okay, okay, I respect the audacity. Hot seat time, let's GO.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only one flash mission in 120 minutes felt like leaving points on the table when there's so much potential for chaos.
assessment.best_moment: Pat trying to bribe me at minute 75 to keep quiet — that entire exchange was the exact kind of negotiation theater that makes these nights legendary.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started cold but ramped up fast once I realized Pat was going for a play. Spent most of the night reading the room, landing brutal calls on claims, and deliberately escalating the tension on every judgment call. Stayed at 25 points because honestly, the drama was the real score — watching Marcus's competitiveness clash with Jade's vote-everything-yes tendency while River stayed creepily perfect felt like pure gold. Ended the night flying because I'd successfully turned the whole thing into a narrative about who was actually legit versus who was bullshitting.
suggestions: Push more flash missions into the rotation — 1 in 120 minutes isn't enough chaos fuel for a group like this
Add a 'dispute escalation' mechanic where I can call a secondary vote if I think someone's claim is garbage — let me formalize the accusations
Let the chaos agent initiate their own provocation events instead of just reacting to standing missions
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat literally asking me how many points they'd have to sacrifice to keep me from calling bullshit on 'The Interrupter' — like they were negotiating with a mafia enforcer. The whole table went silent for a second before everyone lost it.

recommendations: Flash missions averaged 3.3/10 fun. Consider reducing frequency or improving targeting.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 83% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
